title: Agent Coordination Batch Blocker v1
description: A structured blocker persisted on a batch when a supervisor blocks on operator authority, so the dashboard renders the Blocker panel (message, required decisions, recommended reply) instead of reconstructing decisions from lane blockedOn dependencies. The dashboard-rendered blocker payload uses the dashboard's field names (including camelCase recommendedReply); the record envelope (schema_version, batch_id) and contract metadata keep this repository's snake_case convention. This schema defines state only; capture, persistence, and UI behavior are separate concerns.

x-storage-key.segment_encoding: UTF-8 bytes; leave ASCII alphanumeric, underscore, and hyphen literal; percent-encode every other byte with uppercase hexadecimal, so no segment can form a . or .. traversal component. Not wired to the CLI or Worker yet.

workspace.description: Tenant/workspace dimension required by ADR 0004. Self-hosted producers use default. Part of the logical key so two tenants sharing a batch_id do not overwrite each other's blocker.

blocker.description: The structured blocker the dashboard renders as a panel.

message.description: Why the batch is blocked on operator authority.

decisions.description: The required operator decisions. A structured blocker asserts at least one decision; an empty list is not meaningful and should degrade to the dashboard's blockedOn-derived fallback instead.

recommendedReply.description: Optional suggested operator reply. There is exactly one way to signal no recommendation — omit the property — so recommendedReply is never null or empty.
